query: You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository. The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 441a9c92). ## Issue to Resolve Bug on parameter's class that reached maximum line ## Expected Behavior No error. ## Observed Behavior Error. ## Steps to Reproduce I have parameter lambda code like this, let say my parameter is reached maximum limit of line : There is error detected on `open bracket (`. I already clicked `Format with ktlint`, but still error. ## Your Environment * Version of ktlint used: 0.43.2 * Version of Gradle used (if applicable): AGP 7 * Operating System and version: MacOS Big Sur ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Next time, please post your code instead of an image. It is more convenient when trying to reproduce a problem. The code below is comparable to your example: ``` // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right -> | val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null ``` The `ktlint --verbose --relative` command results in: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:1: Exceeded max line length (80) (cannot be auto-corrected) (max-line-length) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:13: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:25: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:37: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:49: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:61: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:73: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:83: Missing newline before ")" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` From this it can be seen that both the `max-line-length` and the `parameter-list-wrapping` report violations in the original code. Auto-correct by the `parameter-list-wrapping` resolves the max line length violation and results in code below: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` but that same rule immediately reports now another lint error as you reported as well: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:3:5: Unnecessary newline before "(" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` So far, I have found no way to refactor the code manually in such a way that the code is accepted by both *ktlint* as well as by the IntelliJ default code formatting which nowadays is a requirement when we make (bugfixes on) rules. For example, the previous code sample will be transformed by IntelliJ default formatting to following: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` This is horrible code. For now, I see no other way for you to disable both rules and stick with one really long line: ``` val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null // ktlint-disable max-line-length parameter-list-wrapping ``` Maybe, someone else has a briljant idea how to the resulting code should look like in such a way that both ktlint as well as IntelliJ default formatting do not alter that code. Next step would then be to fix the rules do that the code will be formatted as such. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `CHANGELOG.md` - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 15→class ParameterListWrappingRuleTest {

 48→    }

content.thinking:
**Considering max-line-length issues**

I'm running into a max-line-length error with some long parameters that don't wrap correctly. In a discussion, after applying nullable type wrapping, the inner list is still exceeding the line length. I’m realizing there isn't a separate rule for multiline max line length checks! So, it looks like I need to wrap the inner list too. This means I can’t skip wrapping function types inside nullable types if they're exceeding the max length. The check for isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType might be too broad; it needs a clearer condition.**Analyzing nullable type wrapping**

I want the nullable type wrapping to allow a newline before the inner left parenthesis (LPAR) without causing issues in parameter list wrapping. This means the wrapParameterList should continue and not eliminate whitespace. Alternatively, I could adjust the nullable type wrapper to keep the inner LPAR on the same line as the outer LPAR when wrapping is needed. But, the wrapper processes nodes in AST order, so it triggers before reaching the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST nodes. If it adds a newline after LPAR, then the later wrapping will see that newline and raise an issue.**Revising wrapNullableType logic**

I could change wrapNullableType so it doesn’t add a newline after the left parenthesis (LPAR) if the function type parameter list will be wrapped. However, I can’t know for sure until I check if the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST itself exceeds the max line length, which it likely does. If that’s the case, I should leave the inner LPAR on the same line without inserting a newline after the outer LPAR. But then there's a risk that the outer type may still exceed the max length, especially if the nullable type node is involved.**Refining line length strategy**

I’m realizing that the line length violation affects the entire variable declaration, but if I keep the outer type on the same line and just wrap the parameters, the declaration might still exceed the max length. Actually, breaking parameters into new lines will help reduce the length—making the first line shorter. So yes, a max line length situation could be resolved without needing to wrap the nullable type if I'm wrapping the inner list instead. However, if this doesn’t work due to disabled parameter list wrapping or very high max line lengths, I’ll need a better strategy. I should adjust needToWrapParameterList to not exclude function types automatically wrapped in nullable types.**Adjusting function type wrapping logic**

I think I should only exclude wrapping if a function type is already wrapped by a nullable type and that wrapping has been applied. The exclusion likely prevents being flagged for "unnecessary newline before ('('" when the nullable wrapper added a newline. However, I can tweak how I handle the left parenthesis (LPAR) to ignore that newline instead of skipping the wrapping altogether. It’s more effective to base decisions on whether the parameter list exceeds max line length in the current context. 

So, here’s the plan: if the function type is wrapped in a nullable type, I’ll check these conditions. If the parameter list is already multiline, I keep it as is. If it exceeds max line length, I wrap it; otherwise, I won't. I noticed that the isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType function may always return false, so I'll implement this new logic and ensure it can recognize nullable types, even with nested parentheses. Time to inspect the element types list for PARENTHESIZED_TYPE!

**Exploring type wrapping in Kotlin**

I'm considering how `ElementType.PARENTHESIZED` could be used for parentheses around types, particularly in Kotlin where it uses `KtNodeTypes.PARENTHESIZED`. So, when wrapping a function type, it could indeed go from `VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST` to `FUNCTION_TYPE`, then to `PARENTHESIZED`, and finally `NULLABLE_TYPE`. 

However, I'm also realizing that `wrapNullableType` might not find `FUNCTION_TYPE` as a direct child if it's wrapped in `PARENTHESIZED`. The test case indicates it should work, but I need to see if outer parentheses in nullable types represent a `PARENTHESIZED` node inside `NULLABLE_TYPE`. Checking the parse tree could clarify, but it sounds complex!**Analyzing Kotlin's nullable type structure**

I’m inferring that NULLABLE_TYPE can wrap outer parentheses directly, since it has LPAR and RPAR as part of its children. This suggests that function types might be inside those parentheses, possibly treated as PARENTHESIZED. However, Kotlin's grammar shows that a nullable type is expressed as `type ?`. While function types need parentheses when nullable, those parentheses aren't part of the NULLABLE_TYPE node.

It seems the actual AST structure could have NULLABLE_TYPE as having both LPAR and RPAR as direct children, meaning the grammar possibly incorporates the parentheses within NULLABLE_TYPE instead of separating them out. Interesting!**Refining nullable type handling**

I'm thinking about how element types suggest there might be a pathway to handle this more elegantly. The tests indicate that `wrapNullableType` effectively works within the AST, so we might stick with the current approach.

I need to ensure that `isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType` can return true even when there are intermediate PARENTHESIZED nodes. I'm also looking to allow wrapping the parameter list if it exceeds the maximum line length, even within nullable types. Finally, I want to prevent the "Unnecessary newline before" warning if the preceding token is the opening parenthesis of the nullable type. That'll be important!**Adjusting LPAR handling in nullable types**

In `needToWrapParameterList`, if we're dealing with a function type in a nullable context and there's a newline before the opening parenthesis (LPAR), we want to still wrap but avoid emitting a newline right before it. 

To manage this, I could adjust the LPAR branch in `wrapParameterInList`—if the LPAR follows a newline due to a nullable wrapper, we shouldn't emit that newline. 

However, rather than removing the LPAR branch completely, which catches dangling parentheses in declarations, I need to ensure it's context-aware. Specifically, we want to remove the newline if it's part of the "dangling opening parenthesis" situation, keeping the LPAR on the same line as the function name or class. It’s a balancing act!**Refining handling of nullable function types**

When dealing with nullable function types formatted like this: `var x: ((a: ..., b: ...) -> Unit)? = null`, the inner opening parenthesis (LPAR) is acceptable when preceded by a newline. We shouldn't raise a flag for "Unnecessary newline before (", especially if it's due to the outer nullable type. To address this, I need to modify how `wrapParameterInList` processes LPAR when it detects a newline before it. 

We want to allow wrapping of inner parameter lists without generating unnecessary errors, so I'm considering a method like `isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableTypeAndStartsOnNewLine()`. The core idea is that if the value parameter list is part of a function type within a nullable context, and if there’s a newline immediately preceding the opening LPAR, we need to treat this differently. I'm also thinking of checking the node's ancestors to confirm it's within a NULLABLE_TYPE, ensuring we handle dangling parentheses cases properly while avoiding unnecessary flags for valid formatting scenarios. It’s a balancing act, but we can get there with careful logic refinement!**Clarifying nullable function type handling**

I'm thinking through how to check for a NULLABLE_TYPE ancestor in the parameter list of a function type. I need to ensure that if the function type is nested in a nullable type, it’s recognized correctly. If we find a NULLABLE_TYPE in the ancestor chain of a FUNCTION_TYPE, it confirms proper wrapping.

I want to modify `needToWrapParameterList` so that it checks for existing newlines first. If there's already a newline or if it exceeds the maximum line length, we want to wrap it, regardless of nullable type conditions. This adjustment should allow for better formatting when function types are aligned. I need to be mindful of how whitespace affects the overall formatting, but I think these changes will streamline the process!**Refining nullable type wrapping**

I'm considering how to handle newline issues for outer parentheses when working with nullable function types. To avoid unnecessary newlines, we should treat them as acceptable when the preceding code leaf is a parenthesis. However, I need to ensure that the indentation stays correct when parameters are wrapped. 

To maintain clarity, I think we shouldn't insert newlines after outer parentheses when wrapping nullable types but keep them adjacent. If the inner parameter list exceeds the maximum line length, we'll handle wrapping at that stage without altering the outer structure. I'll refine the logic for when to wrap based on these considerations to ensure everything lines up correctly!**Addressing line length and formatting**

I'm pondering how the maximum line length rule interacts with parameter list wrapping. The max-line-length rule may not get applied correctly since it runs independently, and it can report violations even if parameter list wrapping could fix them. 

To ensure that our formatting produces valid code, I think we need to add tests for this, particularly for nested parentheses and multiple parameters. I want to set a maximum line length property and ensure our function type parameter lists wrap correctly without raising lint errors afterward. 

I'll refine checks in the relevant methods to make sure they correctly handle these cases!**Clarifying type hierarchy**

I'm considering the hierarchy of function types and nullable types. A function type could indeed be a child of NULLABLE_TYPE, which is a child of TYPE_REFERENCE. However, if there's a TYPE_REFERENCE between the function type and NULLABLE_TYPE, that could complicate things.

To address this, I think I should check the ancestors of the function type to see if any are NULLABLE_TYPE, stopping at TYPE_REFERENCE or PROPERTY. I need to ensure this is correctly implemented and also tweak the wrapping and parameter list functions to ensure they consider line length when formatting. Let's get to the edits!

        when {
            hasNoParameters() -> false
            codeStyle != ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() -> false
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression() ->
                false

            textContains('\n') -> true
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true
            exceedsMaxLineLength() -> true
            else -> false
        }
","new_string":"    private fun ASTNode.needToWrapParameterList() =
        when {
            hasNoParameters() -> false
            codeStyle != ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() -> false
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression() ->
                false
            // When the parameter list is already multiline then keep it multiline.
            textContains('\n') -> true
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true
            exceedsMaxLineLength() -> true
            // Skip wrapping of function type parameter list when it is wrapped in a nullable type and it fits on a single line.
            // This prevents an unnecessary additional wrap inside nullable function types.
            isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType() -> false
            else -> false
        }
"}

function.arguments:
{"file_path":"/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test.kt","content":"package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test {
    private val parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat =
        assertThatRule(
            provider = { ParameterListWrappingRule() },
            additionalRuleProviders =
                setOf(
                    // Apply the IndentationRule always as additional rule, so that the formattedCode in the unit test looks
                    // correct.
                    RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
                ),
        )

    @Test
    fun `Given a nullable function type with a parameter list exceeding the max line length then wrap the parameters without introducing a dangling opening parenthesis`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            val foo1: ((
                s1: String,
                s2: String,
                s3: String,
                s4: String,
                s5: String,
                s6: String
            ) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(
                LintViolation(1, 13, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 25, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 37, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 49, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 61, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 73, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 83, "Missing newline before \")\""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}
"}

The error arises from nested parentheses being placed on their own lines, which I think we can fix by preventing the outer wrap when the inner parentheses exceed a certain length. However, I'm still concerned about cases with long variable names or lengthy parameter lists, as they might cause line length violations even with the changes. This could lead to more issues.**Revising wrapping strategy**

I realize we can’t just skip the outer wrap when the VPL exceeds. Instead, I need to check if wrapping the parameter list will resolve the max-line-length issue for the first line as well. If the variable name is already over the maximum length, we can't fix it unless we break before the colon, which complicates things. The wrapNullableType function works specifically with the NULLABLE_TYPE node, but I need to account for how that interacts with long variable names.**Refining the wrapping logic**

I’m noticing that even if we wrap the parameter list later, the first line with `var longName: ((` might still exceed maximum length if the column at the type start plus 2 is greater than the limit. The parameter list wrap keeps `((` on the same line, but if the starting column is already too long, we still have an issue. I think wrapNullableType should trigger when the type start column exceeds the maximum length or if the prefix is too lengthy, which could avoid those inner newline errors.**Evaluating node wrapping conditions**

I see that we already compute whether the line exceeds maximum length by using `node.column - 1 + node.textLength`. However, we still need to determine if wrapping just the parameter list will fix things. Though wrapping moves text to new lines, it keeps the outer `((` on the same line as the colon, reducing the line length to `prefix + ((`. If `node.column` is large, we might still exceed the limit, so we need to adjust our approach in wrapNullableType, considering specific length calculations for the overall line.**Simplifying wrap conditions**

For a maximum length of 80, if `node.column - 1 + 2 <= 80`, the parameter list wrap is enough. If not, we may need to wrap the nullable type after the outer parenthesis, which moves the inner `(` to a new line. However, if the inner line still exceeds max length due to a long variable name, just wrapping after the colon won't help either. Instead, we should adjust wrapNullableType to apply when needed, ensuring the outer parentheses stay on the same line with proper handling for lengthy prefixes to avoid conflicts.**Adjusting wrap logic for nullable types**

I realize wrapNullableType operates on the NULLABLE_TYPE node itself, with `node.column` representing the starting column of the nullable type's parenthesis. To check if `node.column - 1 + 2` meets the maximum line length, I’m modifying the skip condition. If the parameter list exceeds the max length but there's room for `((`, we don't need to wrap the nullable type. I also need to adjust the wrapParameterInList logic to prevent errors when newlines occur before the inner parenthesis in scenarios that include both wrapping parts. This solution will ensure correct parentheses alignment.

content.thinking:
**Evaluating lint checks**

I realize that `isFormattedAs` only compares formatting output and doesn't check for linting on formatted code. So, I think we can just use `.isFormattedAs(formattedCode)` without worrying about brittle lint violations. But what if we want to ensure there are no lingering errors? In the fix context, they care about lint errors, but this test doesn't. I could also add a second assertion to check for lint violations on the formatted code. Let's plan to edit the test accordingly!**Testing inner wrap scenarios**

I want to create code that needs an inner wrap even after an outer wrap. To test the LPAR newline exception, this situation matters only if the inner wrap triggers while an outer newline exists. This will happen if our `wrapNullableType` runs due to a long line, causing `needToWrapParameterList` to kick in even after the outer wrap. I'll make it extreme with six parameters and very long type names. After the outer wrap, we'll still exceed the line length, prompting the inner wrap!**Constructing parameters for wrapping**

I’ll use four parameters with the type name `VeryLongTypeName`, setting each with a lengthy length of 30. Let's go with `VeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongTypeName`, which is 32 characters long. When I multiply by four and add the parameter names, the total exceeds 120 characters. 

The expected formatted code should look like this:  
```
val foo1: (
    (
        s1: VeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongTypeName,
        s2: VeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongTypeName,
        s3: VeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongTypeName,
        s4: VeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongTypeName
    ) -> Unit
)? = null
```

I'll double-check how the indentation rules apply for the inner and outer parameters.**Updating the test file**

I need to update the test file with a couple of changes. First, I'll call `.withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)`. Then, I’ll use `parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(formattedCode).withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80).hasNoLintViolations()` to ensure no further issues arise. It's interesting to note that `.hasNoLintViolations()` also checks formatting, indicating that when linting succeeds without errors, the formatting gets checked too. So, let's go ahead and make those edits!

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
  9→
 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}
169→

content.text:
Implemented a fix in `ParameterListWrappingRule` for nullable function types where wrapping could introduce an incorrect `Unnecessary newline before "("` violation.

### Root cause
When a nullable function type is wrapped to a new line, the inner function type’s `(` can legitimately start on a new line. The rule incorrectly treated that newline as a “dangling opening parenthesis” and reported `Unnecessary newline before "("`. Also, for nullable function types, the rule decided to wrap the parameter list based on the *current* column (before the nullable-type wrap), causing unnecessary parameter wrapping and extra violations.

### Fix
- Detect nullable-wrapped function types more reliably by skipping intermediate `PARENTHESIZED` nodes.
- For nullable function types, decide whether the parameter list needs wrapping based on whether it would still exceed `max_line_length` **after** the nullable-type wrap indentation is applied.
- Suppress the “dangling opening parenthesis” violation for the inner `(` when it is preceded by the nullable-type wrapping `(`.

### Changes made
- Updated: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`
- Added new test file: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test.kt`

 15→class ParameterListWrappingRuleTest {
 16→    private val parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat =
 17→        assertThatRule(
 18→            provider = { ParameterListWrappingRule() },
 19→            additionalRuleProviders =
 20→                setOf(
 21→                    // Apply the IndentationRule always as additional rule, so that the formattedCode in the unit test looks
 22→                    // correct.
 23→                    RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
 24→                ),
 25→        )
 26→
 27→    @Test
 28→    fun `Given a class with parameters on multiple lines then put each parameter and closing parenthesis on a separate line`() {
 29→        val code =
 30→            """
 31→            class ClassA(paramA: String, paramB: String,
 32→                         paramC: String)
 33→            """.trimIndent()
 34→        val formattedCode =
 35→            """
 36→            class ClassA(
 37→                paramA: String,
 38→                paramB: String,
 39→                paramC: String
 40→            )
 41→            """.trimIndent()
 42→        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 43→            .hasLintViolations(
 44→                LintViolation(1, 14, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
 45→                LintViolation(1, 30, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
 46→                LintViolation(2, 28, """Missing newline before ")""""),
 47→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    @Test
 51→    fun `Given a single line class header which exceeds the maximum line length`() {
 52→        val code =
 53→            """
 54→            class ClassA(paramA: String, paramB: String, paramC: String)
 55→            """.trimIndent()
 56→        val formattedCode =
 57→            """
 58→            class ClassA(
 59→                paramA: String,
 60→                paramB: String,
 61→                paramC: String
 62→            )
 63→            """.trimIndent()
 64→        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
 65→            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 10)
 66→            .hasLintViolations(
 67→                LintViolation(1, 14, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
 68→                LintViolation(1, 30, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
 69→                LintViolation(1, 46, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
 70→                LintViolation(1, 60, """Missing newline before ")""""),
 71→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    @Test
 75→    fun `Given a class header with a very long name and without parameters which exceeds the maximum line length then do not change the class header`() {
 76→        val code =

 82→            .hasNoLintViolations()
 83→    }
 84→

 86→    fun `Given a single line class header with parameters which is formatted correctly then do not change the class header`() {
 87→        val code =
 88→            """
 89→            class ClassA(paramA: String, paramB: String, paramC: String)
 90→            """.trimIndent()
 91→        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    @Test
 95→    fun `Given a multiline class header with parameters which is formatted correctly then do not change the class header`() {
 96→        val code =
 97→            """
 98→            class ClassA(
 99→                paramA: String,
100→                paramB: String,
101→                paramC: String
102→            )
103→            """.trimIndent()
104→        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
105→    }
106→
107→    @Test
108→    fun `Given a multiline function with parameters then start each parameter and closing parenthesis on a separate line`() {
109→        val code =

","new_string":"    private fun ASTNode.needToWrapParameterList() =
        when {
            hasNoParameters() -> false
            codeStyle != ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralInNonKtlintOfficialCodeStyle() -> false
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression() ->
                false
            // When the parameter list is already multiline then keep it multiline.
            textContains('\n') -> true
            codeStyle == ktlint_official && containsAnnotatedParameter() -> true
            exceedsMaxLineLength() -> true
            // Skip wrapping of function type parameter list when it is wrapped in a nullable type and it fits on a single line.
            // This prevents an unnecessary additional wrap inside nullable function types.
            isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType() -> false
            else -> false
        }
"}

{"file_path":"/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test.kt","content":"package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class ParameterListWrappingRuleIssue1324Test {
    private val parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat =
        assertThatRule(
            provider = { ParameterListWrappingRule() },
            additionalRuleProviders =
                setOf(
                    // Apply the IndentationRule always as additional rule, so that the formattedCode in the unit test looks
                    // correct.
                    RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
                ),
        )

    @Test
    fun `Given a nullable function type with a parameter list exceeding the max line length then wrap the parameters without introducing a dangling opening parenthesis`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            val foo1: ((
                s1: String,
                s2: String,
                s3: String,
                s4: String,
                s5: String,
                s6: String
            ) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(
                LintViolation(1, 13, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 25, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 37, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 49, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 61, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 73, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 83, "Missing newline before \")\""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}
"}

 51→/**
 52→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 53→ *
 54→ * ```
 55→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 56→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()

 58→ * @Test
 59→ * fun `Some test`() {
 60→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 61→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted

 64→ *         .isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
 65→ * }
 66→ * ```
 67→ */
 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(
 70→    /**
 71→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.

 93→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
 94→     */
 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this

100→
101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
103→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
104→     * silently overwritten.
105→     *
106→     * Example of usage:
107→     * ```
108→     *  val code =
109→     *      """
110→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
111→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
112→     *      """.trimIndent()
113→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
114→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
115→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
116→     * ```
117→     */
118→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
119→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
120→        code
121→            .split("\n")
122→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
123→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
124→            ?.let { index -> editorConfigProperties.add(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString()) }

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
  9→
 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}
